title: Special military aircraft from Middle East at Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The United Arab Emirates Air Force C-17A Globemaster passed by our photographer (Image: Charlie Gilmour) A SPECIAL military aircraft from the Middle East has been spotted at Prestwick Airport. The United Arab Emirates Air Force C-17A Globemaster passed by our photographer Charlie Gilmour. The aircraft has been in service since 2012 and was painted in its gold colour last year.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The United Arab Emirates Air Force C-17A Globemaster passed by our photographer Charlie Gilmour (Image: Charlie Gilmour) It is a regular sight at Prestwick, and is also well-known from appearances at the Dubai Airshow. It is unique from most C-17 military aircraft which are normally grey in colour.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Boeing C-17 Globemaster III is mostly used for rapid strategic airlift of troops and cargo to main operating bases or forward bases anywhere in the world.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Special military aircraft from Middle East spotted at Prestwick Airport
